---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---


```{r}
###############################################################################
##  Gallus gallus ID-mapping helper for ExpressAnalyst / iDEP / DAVID
##  (c) 2025  Galen O’Shea-Stone
##  Dependencies:  Bioconductor (biomaRt ≥ 2.56.1), dplyr ≥ 1.1
###############################################################################

## 1.  PACKAGES  --------------------------------------------------------------
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

required_pkgs <- c("biomaRt", "dplyr", "readr", "stringr", "tidyr")

for (pkg in required_pkgs) {
    if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE))
        BiocManager::install(pkg, ask = FALSE)
}

library(biomaRt)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)

## 2.  USER INPUT -------------------------------------------------------------
# Path to the raw count matrix exported from FeatureCounts/HTSeq
infile  <- "ExpAnalyst_format.csv"        # <- change if needed
outfile_counts <- "counts_entrez.csv"
outfile_map    <- "id_map_table.csv"

## 3.  READ & PRE-CLEAN  ------------------------------------------------------
message("Reading count matrix …")
counts_raw <- read_csv(infile, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Assume first column holds Ensembl IDs (may include version suffix)
id_col <- colnames(counts_raw)[1]

# Drop ERCC, no_feature, etc. (safe even if not present)
bad_patterns <- c("^ERCC", "^__")
counts <- counts_raw %>%
    filter(!str_detect(.data[[id_col]], str_c(bad_patterns, collapse = "|")))

# Remove “.1”, “.2” version suffixes
counts[[id_col]] <- str_remove(counts[[id_col]], "\\.\\d+$")

# Convert transcript IDs (ENSGALT…) → gene IDs (ENSGALG…)
counts[[id_col]] <- str_replace(counts[[id_col]],
                                "^ENSGALT(\\d{9})$", "ENSGALG\\1")

## 4.  CONNECT TO Ensembl  ----------------------------------------------------
message("Connecting to Ensembl biomart …")
mart <- useEnsembl(biomart = "genes",
                   dataset = "ggallus_gene_ensembl",
                   mirror  = "uswest")   # pick the closest mirror; change if blocked

## 5.  GET MAPPING TABLE  -----------------------------------------------------
message("Querying mapping for ", nrow(counts), " unique IDs …")
map_tbl <- getBM(
    attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id",
                   
                   "entrezgene_id",
                   "external_gene_name"),
    filters    = "ensembl_gene_id",
    values     = counts[[id_col]],
    mart       = mart
) %>%
    rename(!!id_col := ensembl_gene_id,
           ENTREZ_ID = entrezgene_id,
           GENE_SYM  = external_gene_name) %>%
    distinct()

## 6.  MERGE & TIDY  ----------------------------------------------------------
counts_mapped <- counts %>%
    left_join(map_tbl, by = id_col) %>%
    relocate(ENTREZ_ID, .before = 1)

# Report mapping success
n_total   <- nrow(counts)
n_mapped  <- sum(!is.na(counts_mapped$ENTREZ_ID))
pct_map   <- round(n_mapped / n_total * 100, 1)
message("Mapped ", n_mapped, " of ", n_total, " rows (", pct_map, " %).")

# Remove rows still lacking Entrez ID
counts_ok <- counts_mapped %>%
    filter(!is.na(ENTREZ_ID))

# Collapse duplicate Entrez IDs (sum counts OR take mean – pick one)
collapse_fun <- dplyr::summarise  # change if you prefer mean()
counts_ok <- counts_ok %>%
    group_by(ENTREZ_ID) %>%
    collapse_fun(across(where(is.numeric), sum)) %>%
    ungroup()

## 7.  WRITE OUTPUTS  ---------------------------------------------------------
message("Writing cleaned counts → ", outfile_counts)
write_csv(counts_ok %>% select(ENTREZ_ID, where(is.numeric)),
          outfile_counts)

message("Writing mapping table  → ", outfile_map)
write_csv(map_tbl, outfile_map)

message("All done!  Ready to upload ‘", outfile_counts, "’ to ExpressAnalyst.")

## 6b.  OPTIONAL — map annotations back to original rows  --------------------
##      + add gene symbols to the collapsed table
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# -----(A) full row-level copy, just annotated)---------------------------------
outfile_counts_annot_rows <- "counts_annotated_rows.csv"

counts_annot_rows <- counts_mapped %>%                      # from Step 6
    relocate(ENTREZ_ID, GENE_SYM, .before = 1)              # bring new cols forward

write_csv(counts_annot_rows, outfile_counts_annot_rows)
message("Wrote annotated row-level copy → ", outfile_counts_annot_rows)

# -----(B) add gene symbols to the collapsed counts_ok table)------------------
outfile_counts_sym <- "counts_entrez_symbol.csv"

# build a simple lookup of one symbol per ENTREZ_ID
gene_sym_lookup <- map_tbl %>%                    # from Step 5
    filter(!is.na(GENE_SYM) & GENE_SYM != "") %>% # keep useful entries
    distinct(ENTREZ_ID, GENE_SYM) %>%             # remove dupes
    group_by(ENTREZ_ID) %>%
    slice_head(n = 1) %>%                         # take first symbol if many
    ungroup()

counts_ok_sym <- counts_ok %>%                    # from Step 6
    left_join(gene_sym_lookup, by = "ENTREZ_ID") %>%
    relocate(GENE_SYM, .after = ENTREZ_ID)

write_csv(counts_ok_sym, outfile_counts_sym)
message("Wrote collapsed counts with symbols → ", outfile_counts_sym)

## 6c.  SUMMARY STATISTICS  ----------------------------------------------------
##      – mapping success + collapsed genes + library-size overview
## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
outfile_summary <- "id_mapping_summary.tsv"

# ---- core mapping numbers ----------------------------------------------------
total_rows_input        <- n_total                    # from Step 6
rows_with_entrez        <- n_mapped
rows_without_entrez     <- total_rows_input - rows_with_entrez
unique_entrez_after_collapse <- nrow(counts_ok)       # collapsed table
duplicates_collapsed    <- rows_with_entrez - unique_entrez_after_collapse

# ---- per-sample library sizes (after collapsing) -----------------------------
sample_cols <- counts_ok %>% select(where(is.numeric)) %>% colnames()
lib_sizes   <- colSums(counts_ok[sample_cols])

# ---- assemble summary table --------------------------------------------------
summary_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
  Metric = c(
    "Timestamp",
    "Rows in input (post-filtering)",
    "Rows with Entrez mapping",
    "Rows without Entrez mapping",
    "Unique Entrez IDs after collapsing",
    "Duplicates collapsed",
    "Number of samples (columns)",
    "Mean library size",
    "Median library size",
    "Min library size",
    "Max library size"
  ),
  Value = c(
    format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),
    total_rows_input,
    rows_with_entrez,
    rows_without_entrez,
    unique_entrez_after_collapse,
    duplicates_collapsed,
    length(sample_cols),
    round(mean(lib_sizes), 1),
    median(lib_sizes),
    min(lib_sizes),
    max(lib_sizes)
  )
)

# ---- write & display ---------------------------------------------------------
readr::write_tsv(summary_tbl, outfile_summary)
print(summary_tbl, n = Inf)   # shows everything in console

message("Wrote summary statistics → ", outfile_summary)



```
